---
title: pipenv
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r knitr_config, code=readLines("_knitr_config.R"), echo=FALSE}
```

<!-- ********************************************************************* -->

<!-- > 'preferred installer program' the standard package manager for python -->

*********************************************************************

## links 

* https://realpython.com/pipenv-guide/

* https://www.jetbrains.com/help/pycharm/pipenv.html

<!-- ********************************************************************* -->

<!-- ## faqs -->

<!-- * [upgrade pip](#upgrade-pip)   -->
<!-- * [install packages from requirements.txt](#requirements)   -->

*********************************************************************

## setup

### install

```sh
pip install pipenv
```

*********************************************************************

## basics

```sh
# create a virtual environment
pipenv shell

# install a package into your virtual environment
pipenv install pandas

# install a package into your virtual environment just for dev purposes
pipenv install pytest --dev

# automatically detect requirements.txt and convert to a Pipfile
pipenv install

# lock your environment - create Pipfile.lock
pipenv lock

# install from Pipfile.lock
pipenv install --ignore-pipfile

# find out where your virtual environment is
pipenv --venv

# find out where your project home is
pipenv --where
```

*********************************************************************

## files

### Pipfile

Contains top-level dependences. E.g. 

```
[[source]]
url = "https://pypi.python.org/simple"
verify_ssl = true
name = "pypi"

[dev-packages]
pytest = "*"

[packages]
flask = "==0.12.1"
numpy = "*"
requests = {git = "https://github.com/requests/requests.git", editable = true}

[requires]
python_version = "3.6"
```

### Pipfile.lock

Don't need to edit this manually - it gets made when you run `pipenv lock`
